description: Windows APIs that commonly trigger EDR/AV detection when used in suspicious patterns

process_injection.description: APIs used for process injection techniques

apis.name: VirtualAllocEx
apis.description: Allocates memory in another process. When combined with WriteProcessMemory + CreateRemoteThread = classic injection pattern.
detection_patterns: VirtualAllocEx → WriteProcessMemory → CreateRemoteThread sequence
PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE permissions on cross-process allocation
apis.evasion_notes: Use VirtualProtectEx to change from RW to RX after writing. Consider alternative allocation methods.

apis.name: WriteProcessMemory
apis.description: Writes data to another process memory space.
detection_patterns: Writing executable code patterns to other processes
Large writes to recently allocated memory

apis.name: CreateRemoteThread
apis.description: Creates thread in another process. Heavily monitored by EDR.
detection_patterns: Thread creation pointing to recently written memory
Threads created in system processes (explorer.exe, winlogon.exe)

apis.name: NtMapViewOfSection
apis.description: Maps section into process address space. Used in advanced injection.
detection_patterns: Mapping executable sections into other processes

apis.name: QueueUserAPC
apis.description: Alternative to CreateRemoteThread, queues APC to thread.
detection_patterns: APC queued to threads in other processes
APC pointing to shellcode-like memory

apis.name: VirtualAlloc
apis.description: Allocates virtual memory. RWX allocations are suspicious.
detection_patterns: PAGE_EXECUTE_READWRITE allocations
Large allocations (>1MB) with execute permissions
apis.evasion_notes: Allocate as RW, write shellcode, then VirtualProtect to RX

apis.name: VirtualProtect
apis.description: Changes memory protection. Less suspicious than RWX allocation.
detection_patterns: Changing memory from RW to RX on heap allocations
Frequent protection changes on same memory region

apis.name: HeapCreate
apis.description: Creates private heap with executable permissions.
detection_patterns: HEAP_CREATE_ENABLE_EXECUTE flag usage

process_manipulation.description: APIs for process creation and manipulation

apis.name: CreateProcessA/W
apis.description: Creates new process. Suspicious when combined with injection.
detection_patterns: CREATE_SUSPENDED flag with subsequent injection
Creating processes with unusual parent-child relationships

apis.name: OpenProcess
detection_patterns: PROCESS_ALL_ACCESS or high-privilege access to system processes
Opening multiple processes in sequence (process enumeration)

apis.name: ResumeThread
apis.description: Resumes suspended thread. Part of process hollowing.
detection_patterns: Resuming threads after memory modifications

anti_analysis.description: APIs commonly used for evasion and anti-analysis

apis.name: IsDebuggerPresent
detection_patterns: Anti-debugging checks in malware

apis.name: SetUnhandledExceptionFilter
apis.description: Sets exception handler. Used in anti-debugging.
detection_patterns: Custom exception handlers in suspicious processes

apis.name: OutputDebugStringA/W
apis.description: Can be used for anti-debugging tricks.
detection_patterns: Using OutputDebugString in anti-debug schemes

crypto_apis.description: Cryptography APIs used for payload encryption/obfuscation

apis.name: CryptDecrypt
detection_patterns: Decrypting large amounts of data before execution
Runtime decryption of PE headers

apis.name: CryptAcquireContext
detection_patterns: Crypto operations on executable memory regions
